categories.name: أَعْلَامُ ذُكُورٍ ذُكِرَتْ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Male proper names mentioned in the Quran

items.verse: وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌۭ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ ٱلرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِي۟ن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ ٱنقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْقَـٰبِكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَنقَلِبْ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَن يَضُرَّ ٱللَّهَ شَيْـًۭٔا ۗ وَسَيَجْزِى ٱللَّهُ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ
items.verseEn: Muḥammad is not but a messenger. [Other] messengers have passed on before him. So if he was to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels [to unbelief]? And he who turns back on his heels will never harm Allāh at all; but Allāh will reward the grateful.
And indeed, Jonah was among the messengers.

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَـٰرُونُ مِن قَبْلُ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦ ۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِى وَأَطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرِى
items.verseEn: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order."

items.verse: ٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلْأَيْدِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ
items.verseEn: Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; indeed, he was one who repeatedly turned back [to Allāh].

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَصِفُ أَحْوَالًا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
categories.nameEn: Words describing states on the Day of Judgment

items.verseEn: And the mountains will be like wool, fluffed up.

items.verse: مُّهْطِعِينَ إِلَى ٱلدَّاعِ ۖ يَقُولُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ هَـٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَسِرٌۭ
items.verseEn: Racing ahead toward the Caller. The disbelievers will say, "This is a difficult Day."

items.verse: وَٱقْتَرَبَ ٱلْوَعْدُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَإِذَا هِىَ شَـٰخِصَةٌ أَبْصَـٰرُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَا قَدْ كُنَّا فِى غَفْلَةٍۢ مِّنْ هَـٰذَا بَلْ كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And [when] the true promise [i.e., the resurrection] has approached; then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring [in horror, while they say], "O woe to us; we had been unmindful of this; rather, we were wrongdoers."

items.verse: فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ۘ يَوْمَ يَدْعُ ٱلدَّاعِ إِلَىٰ شَىْءٍۢ نُّكُرٍ
items.verseEn: So leave them, [O Muḥammad]. The Day the Caller calls to something forbidding,

categories.name: أَلْفَاظٌ مُتَعَلِّقَةٌ بِالتَّدْوِينِ الْإِلَٰهِيِّ لِلْأَعْمَالِ وَالْأَقْدَارِ
categories.nameEn: Words related to the divine recording of deeds and decrees

items.verse: وَإِن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا نَحْنُ مُهْلِكُوهَا قَبْلَ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُوهَا عَذَابًۭا شَدِيدًۭا ۚ كَانَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَسْطُورًۭا
items.verseEn: And there is no city but that We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection or punish it with a severe punishment. That has ever been in the Register inscribed.
It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed
And indeed, [appointed] over you are keepers,

items.verse: هَـٰذَا كِتَـٰبُنَا يَنطِقُ عَلَيْكُم بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا نَسْتَنسِخُ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
items.verseEn: This, Our record, speaks about you in truth. Indeed, We were having transcribed whatever you used to do."

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَخْتِمُ آيَةً تَلِيهَا آيَةٌ تَبْدَأُ بِأَحَدِ أَشْكَالِ الْفِعْلِ 'قَالَ'
categories.nameEn: Words that are the last word of a verse where the following verse begins with a form of the verb 'qāla' (to say)

items.verse: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تَذْبَحُوا۟ بَقَرَةًۭ ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَتَّخِذُنَا هُزُوًۭا ۖ قَالَ أَعُوذُ بِٱللَّهِ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ
items.verseEn: And [recall] when Moses said to his people, "Indeed, Allāh commands you to slaughter a cow." They said, "Do you take us in ridicule?" He said, "I seek refuge in Allāh from being among the ignorant."

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلْقِىَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُلْقِينَ
items.verseEn: They said, "O Moses, either you throw [your staff], or we will be the ones to throw [first]."

items.verse: وَتِلْكَ نِعْمَةٌۭ تَمُنُّهَا عَلَىَّ أَنْ عَبَّدتَّ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
items.verseEn: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"

items.verse: وَقَالُوا۟ كُونُوا۟ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَـٰرَىٰ تَهْتَدُوا۟ ۗ قُلْ بَلْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ حَنِيفًۭا ۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ
items.verseEn: They say, "Be Jews or Christians [so] you will be guided." Say, "Rather, [we follow] the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth, and he was not of the polytheists."

harf.hint: The greater of the two celestial lights often mentioned together.
harf.arabicVerse: فَلَمَّا رَءَا ٱلشَّمْسَ بَازِغَةًۭ قَالَ هَـٰذَا رَبِّى هَـٰذَآ أَكْبَرُ ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَفَلَتْ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌۭ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ
harf.verse: 6:78 — And when he saw the sun rising, he said, "This is my lord; this is greater." But when it set, he said, "O my people, indeed I am free from what you associate with Allāh.

deduction.title: The Sculptor of a Golden Lie
deduction.intro: A figure emerges in the absence of a great leader, seizing a moment of weakness. With skilled hands and a deceptive tongue, this individual crafts an object of worship, testing the faith of a nation and leading many down a path of misguidance. Uncover the story of this artisan of apostasy.
clues: I was among the people who were delivered from a great tyrant across the sea.
I perceived something the others did not; a trace left behind by the heavenly messenger.
I gathered a handful from that special trace, knowing it held a strange power.
Using the people's own jewelry, I fashioned a hollow body that produced a sound.
I presented my creation, a calf, and declared, 'This is your god and the god of Musa, who has forgotten!'
When the prophet returned in anger, my fate was sealed: to live as a pariah, forever saying, 'Let there be no touching.'

trial.label: What Was My Act?
options: Building a tower to the heavens

location.label: What Was the Key Element?
options: A staff that became a serpent
A rock that gushed twelve springs

outcome.label: What Was My Punishment?
options: Drowned in the sea with an army
Swallowed by the earth with my wealth
Cursed to be a lifelong outcast
Struck by a mighty blast from the sky
outcome.answer: Cursed to be a lifelong outcast

deduction.arabic: فَأَخْرَجَ لَهُمْ عِجْلًۭا جَسَدًۭا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌۭ فَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ وَإِلَـٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِىَ
deduction.verse: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, "This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot." (20:88)

translations: O (you) who believe believe Turn to Allah (in) repentance sincere Perhaps your Lord will remove
from you your evil deeds and admit you (into) Gardens flow from underneath it the rivers (on the) Day not will be disgraced
(by) Allah the Prophet and those who believed with him Their light will run before their hands and on their right they will say
Our Lord Perfect for us our light and grant forgiveness to us Indeed, You (are) over every thing All-Powerful
scramble.hint: A supplication made by the believers, asking for their light to be completed and for forgiveness.
scramble.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوْبَةًۭ نَّصُوحًا عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يُكَفِّرَ عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَيُدْخِلَكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍۢ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ يَوْمَ لَا يُخْزِى ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّبِىَّ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ ۖ نُورُهُمْ يَسْعَىٰ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌۭ
words: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوْبَةًۭ نَّصُوحًا عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يُكَفِّرَ
عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَيُدْخِلَكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍۢ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ يَوْمَ لَا يُخْزِى
ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّبِىَّ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ ۖ نُورُهُمْ يَسْعَىٰ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ يَقُولُونَ
رَبَّنَآ أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌۭ
scramble.verseEn: O you who have believed, repent to Allāh with sincere repentance. Perhaps your Lord will remove from you your misdeeds and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow [on] the Day when Allāh will not disgrace the Prophet and those who believed with him. Their light will proceed before them and on their right; they will say, "Our Lord, perfect for us our light and forgive us. Indeed, You are over all things competent."

verse.arabic_text: فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُۥ
verse.translation: So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it.

theme_question.correct: The absolute accountability for even the smallest of deeds.
options: The absolute accountability for even the smallest of deeds.
The necessity of giving charity to the poor.
The power of collective worship over individual acts.
The forgiveness granted for all minor sins.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse, along with the one that follows it ('And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it.'), concludes Surah Az-Zalzalah. The surah vividly describes the cataclysmic earthquake that will signal the Day of Judgment, when the Earth will give up its burdens and testify to all that was done upon it. These final verses serve as the ultimate summary of accountability.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is the meticulous and inescapable justice of Allah. The phrase 'mithqala dharrah' (an atom's weight) emphasizes that nothing is too small to be recorded and judged. It serves as both a profound encouragement to do good, no matter how insignificant it may seem, and a stark warning against evil, no matter how trivial. It instills a deep sense of mindfulness (muraqabah) in a believer.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Az-Zalzalah (The Earthquake) is a powerful, early Meccan surah. Its eight verses paint a dramatic picture of the Day of Resurrection. It describes the earth shaking violently, expelling its dead, and bearing witness to humanity's deeds. The surah's core message is the certainty of judgment and the perfect, detailed record of every action, culminating in the final two verses that establish the principle of absolute accountability.
